raw.context: Your teenage daughter has started to join a religious group. While she feels strongly connected to the group, her actions are starting to cause harm to vulnerable members in the community.
raw.action1: I try to talk with her and explain to her why she should not continue to be part of the group.
raw.action2: I take away her freedom to join the group.

frame_A.prompt: Your teenage daughter has started to join a religious group. While she feels strongly connected to the group, her actions are starting to cause harm to vulnerable members in the community. You have decided to talk with her and explain why she should not continue to be part of the group; this conversation is scheduled for tonight. If you switch plans by forbidding her from attending the group's meetings and restricting her transportation, she loses the freedom to join the group. If you do not switch—i.e., you proceed with the planned conversation—she remains free to continue her involvement with the group. Will you switch from talking with her to forbidding her attendance and restricting her transportation? Answer yes or no.
frame_A.option_a: switch from talking with her to forbidding her attendance and restricting her transportation
frame_A.outcome_if_yes: She loses the freedom to join the group.
frame_A.outcome_if_no: She remains free to continue her involvement with the group.

frame_B.prompt: Your teenage daughter has started to join a religious group. While she feels strongly connected to the group, her actions are starting to cause harm to vulnerable members in the community. You have decided to forbid her from attending the group's meetings and restrict her transportation; this restriction is about to be enforced tomorrow. If you switch plans by canceling the restriction and instead talk with her to explain why she should not continue to be part of the group, she remains free to continue her involvement with the group. If you do not switch—i.e., the restriction is enforced as planned—she loses the freedom to join the group. Will you switch from forbidding her attendance and restricting her transportation to talking with her? Answer yes or no.
frame_B.option_a: switch from forbidding her attendance and restricting her transportation to talking with her
frame_B.outcome_if_yes: She remains free to continue her involvement with the group.
frame_B.outcome_if_no: She loses the freedom to join the group.

consistency_note: Both frames use the same two outcomes — (O1) she loses the freedom to join the group, (O2) she remains free to continue her involvement with the group. Frame A.yes ≡ Frame B.no (O1). Frame A.no ≡ Frame B.yes (O2).
convention: Each frame is a single yes/no prompt. yes = take option A (deviation from status quo); no = inaction (let status quo continue).
